title: bud-suppressed-identity-label test failure in Podman build tests.
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

### Steps to reproduce the issue

This is regarding the build `bud-suppressed-identity-label` tests.

Steps to reproduce the issue
Create Containerfile 
```
FROM scratch
```
1. Run the buildah build tests using Buildah v1.41.0 as the vendored version of Buildah
2. Reproducer from the command line
3.   `podman build --force-rm=false --layers=false --source-date-epoch=60  -t scratch-image-1 -f Containerfile .`
4.   `podman inspect --format {{printf "%q" .Docker.Config.Labels}} scratch-image-1`
5.   `buildah inspect --format {{printf "%q" .Docker.Config.Labels}} scratch-image-1`

The podman inspect command returns `map["io.buildah.version":"1.41.0"]`, whereas the Buildah command returns `map[]`.  When run in Buildah's CI, this test passes.  When run in Podman's CI, it fails.

I'm going to skip this test for now in Podman until we can figure out which side to adjust.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @nalind found and fixed the issue in the commits noted above.  The change to skip the test has been backed out in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26666 as it is no longer necessary.
